code.text: Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF)

note.text: Ejection fraction 30% on last echocardiogram (2026-02-10). NYHA Class III.
Monitor INR regularly. Target INR 2.0-3.0 for atrial fibrillation. CAUTION: Patient also on aspirin — increased bleeding risk.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1000mg by mouth twice daily with meals

note.text: WARNING: Patient has CKD Stage 3 with eGFR 38 mL/min. Metformin dose should be reviewed — risk of lactic acidosis with eGFR <45. Consider dose reduction to 500mg BID or discontinuation.
Monitor potassium and creatinine. Note: Potassium currently elevated at 5.3 mEq/L — ACE inhibitor may be contributing.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 40mg by mouth once daily at bedtime

coding.display: Urea nitrogen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

note.text: Above target. Diabetes management needs optimization. Consider medication adjustment given CKD constraints.

coding.display: INR in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay

note.text: Supratherapeutic INR. Increased bleeding risk, especially with concurrent aspirin. Consider warfarin dose adjustment.

coding.display: Glomerular filtration rate/1.73 sq M.predicted [Volume Rate/Area] in Serum or Plasma by Creatinine-based formula (MDRD)

note.text: eGFR 38 mL/min indicates CKD Stage 3b (30-44). Metformin dose adjustment required. Renal dosing review recommended for all medications.
Significantly elevated BNP consistent with active heart failure. Correlate with clinical symptoms and volume status.

coding.display: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

referenceRange.text: <100 mg/dL (optimal for high-risk cardiac patients; target <70 for very high risk)

note.text: Near goal but given ASCVD risk factors (diabetes, CKD, HF), a target of <70 mg/dL may be more appropriate. Consider statin intensification.
